summary: Tested iter25 verify-email bugfix: backend endpoint returns {verified,email,name} without auto-login session cookie, and frontend redirects to landing with prefilled login dialog. All 4 backend pytest cases + 3 frontend Playwright flows PASSED.
critical_code_review_comments: VerifyEmail.jsx useRef guard correctly prevents double API call in StrictMode/dev.
Landing.jsx cleans URL via navigate('/', {replace:true}) so refresh does NOT re-trigger toast/dialog — confirmed via reload test.
EmailAuthDialog.jsx initialEmail useEffect correctly forces mode='login' and clears any leftover notice/successMsg when opened with prefill.

Backend auth_verify_email uses $unset to remove verification_token and verification_expires_at — idempotent on already-verified accounts (else branch), single-use after first success (token gone → 400 'Token inválido o ya usado').
Backend endpoint does NOT call _create_session and does NOT set session_token cookie — confirmed via Set-Cookie header inspection.
test_credentials: Seeded ephemeral users directly in MongoDB (TEST_verify_* and TEST_fe_valid_*) — cleaned up after each test.
seed_data_creation: Each test inserts a fresh user document in `users` collection with auth_provider='password', verification_token (64 chars hex), verification_expires_at (+24h ISO). Cleaned up at the end.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend tests live in /app/backend/tests/test_verify_email_iter25.py — run targeted with pytest -v. Frontend flow uses REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL from /app/frontend/.env (preview URL). For repeatability, seed a verification_token in MongoDB then navigate browser to {BASE}/auth/verify-email/{token}. Verified end-to-end: success screen → 1.8s redirect → /?verified=1&email=... → toast 'Correo verificado' → EmailAuthDialog open in login mode with email prefilled → URL cleaned to '/' (refresh does not re-trigger).
rca of the issue: Not a bug investigation — feature/change validation. Backend correctly removed _create_session() call from auth_verify_email and now returns plain JSON. Frontend correctly handles ?verified=1&email= query params on Landing and passes initialEmail prop into EmailAuthDialog. Behavior matches user requirements exactly.
